name: Cerro del Espino from Raso de la Cruz via Monte Mosomero — circular Sierra de Algairén
name_original: Cerro del Espino desde el Raso de la Cruz por el Monte Mosomero — circular Sierra de Algairén
region_intro: Hiking around Cosuenda offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Algairén, a mountain range characterized by lush forests and impressive geological formations. The region features a mix of pine, holm oak, oak, maple, and wild cherry trees, with ancient slate and quartzite formations. Elevated points provide panoramic views, and routes often lead through picturesque ravines like Valdecerezo and Los Hortales. Best hiking trails around Cosuenda The most popular hiking route is Cerro del Espino from Raso de la Cruz via Monte Mosomero — circular Sierra de Algairén, a 10.7 miles (17.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult circular route explores the varied terrain of the Sierra de Algairén. Another top favourite among local hikers is Aunt's Trail – Picnic Area with Wooden Cabin loop from Albergue de Aguarón, a moderate 8.2 miles (13.2 km) path. This loop takes approximately 4 hours 18 minutes and passes through varied woodlands. Local hikers also love the Aunt's Trail – Picnic Area with Wooden Cabin loop from Parque del Raso de la Cruz, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail leading through diverse woodlands, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Hiking around Cosuenda is defined by the Sierra de Algairén, diverse forests, and panoramic views. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Cosuenda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Cosuenda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there around Cosuenda?
A: Cosuenda offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 25 distinct trails available. These routes cater to different skill levels, ranging from easy strolls to challenging ascents within the Sierra de Algairén.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on hikes in Cosuenda?
A: The terrain around Cosuenda is diverse, characterized by the Sierra de Algairén's lush forests, varied ecosystems, and impressive geological formations. You'll find paths winding through pine, holm oak, and wild cherry trees, often leading through picturesque ravines. Difficulty levels vary, with 3 easy routes, over 20 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes available, ensuring options for all experience levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Cosuenda?
A: Yes, many routes in Cosuenda are circular, offering varied experiences. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975509825/cerro-del-espino-from-raso-de-la-cruz-via-monte-mosomero-circular-sierra-de-algairen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Espino from Raso de la Cruz via Monte Mosomero — circular Sierra de Algairén&lt;/a&gt; explores the varied terrain of the Sierra. Another popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31704830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aunt's Trail – Picnic Area with Wooden Cabin loop from Albergue de Aguarón&lt;/a&gt;, which passes through varied woodlands.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Cosuenda?
A: The Sierra de Algairén is enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with blooming flora or rich autumnal colors. Summer can be warm, but higher elevations provide cooler air. Winter hikes are also possible, offering a different perspective of the serene paths and forests.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in the Sierra de Algairén?
A: The Sierra de Algairén is a natural area, and generally, dogs are welcome on trails. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to follow local regulations regarding waste disposal to protect the rich biodiversity of this Site of Community Importance (LIC).

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Cosuenda?
A: Absolutely. Cosuenda offers several easy and moderate trails suitable for families. These routes often feature gentle elevation changes and pass through diverse woodlands, providing an enjoyable experience for all ages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aunt's Trail – Picnic Area with Wooden Cabin loop from Parque del Raso de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that is often completed in under three hours.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Sierra de Algairén is a haven for wildlife. Hikers might spot wild boars, deer, foxes, and even wildcats. The area is also rich in birdlife, including peregrine falcons and Dartford warblers. Its status as a Natura 2000 network site underscores its significant faunistic wealth.

Q: What are the most scenic viewpoints or natural features to look out for?
A: The Sierra de Algairén offers breathtaking vistas. From the summit of Pico Valdemadera (1,276 meters), you can enjoy expansive views of the Jalón Valley, Moncayo, and even the Pyrenees on clear days. The Mirador de la Falaguera (1177m) also provides spectacular panoramas. Routes often lead through picturesque ravines like Valdecerezo and Los Hortales, showcasing the area's geological diversity.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, there are several points of interest near the hiking trails. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Luz&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4822549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ridge of the Sierra de Algairén&lt;/a&gt; itself is a notable natural feature offering stunning views.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the trailheads in Cosuenda?
A: While Cosuenda is accessible by road, public transport options directly to specific trailheads within the Sierra de Algairén may be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to Cosuenda village and plan for onward travel to trail starting points, such as Raso de la Cruz, which is a common origin for many circular routes.

Q: Where can I park when starting a hike in Cosuenda?
A: Many popular hiking routes in Cosuenda, particularly those ascending the Sierra de Algairén, originate from areas like Raso de la Cruz. These locations typically offer designated parking areas for hikers, providing convenient access to the trails.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Cosuenda?
A: The hiking experiences in Cosuenda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the forests, and the spectacular panoramic views from elevated points like Pico Valdemadera and Mirador de la Falaguera. The well-maintained trails and rich biodiversity are also frequently highlighted.
tips: We find ourselves going up the ravine of the hortales, by the path known as the one of the Tía. It will guide us until we reach the North range of the Sierra de Algairén. Nice path with awesome views when you reach the top.
Located 1038 meters above sea level and on the path of La Lechera, we find this beautiful picture in the Collado de la Hermana with views of the top of Monte del Cortado

Cerro del Espino is located in the center of the Sierra de Algairén, between Almonacid de la Sierra and Cosuenda, which is 50 kilometers from Zaragoza. At the top of this mountain, we will find a metal cross that is usually adorned with handkerchiefs and the typical cachirulo of the land.
